title: 3 Top-Rated Travel Stocks That Analysts Are Loving Now
text: Over the past year, consumers have shifted their spending preferences from goods to services. After a two-year slump due to the pandemic, travel has rebounded strongly and is approaching pre-pandemic levels.

title: 7 Best Travel Stocks to Buy Ahead of Travel Tuesday
text: The search for the best travel stocks is heating up. With September showing an impressive $104 billion in travel spending and a steady 3.5% growth year-to-date (YTD), the sector shows potential for growth even in challenging economic conditions.

title: 3 Eco-Tourism Stocks to Take Advantage of the Conscious Traveler Trend
text: The buzz around buying eco-tourism stocks grows louder as the world increasingly leans toward sustainable travel. Travelers today are more conscious of their environmental footprint, propelling the ecotourism industry into a significant surge.

title: Expedia vs Airbnb stock: Mark Mahaney picks a side
text: Shares of Airbnb Inc (NASDAQ: ABNB) lack a meaningful upside from here now that they have gained a whopping 50% this year, as per a senior Evercore ISI analyst. Mahaney has a $136 price target on Airbnb stock On Friday, Mark Mahaney downgraded Airbnb to “in line”.

title: Travel Stock Swap: Expedia Rises After Analyst Upgrade, Airbnb Downgraded
text: Analysts sees Expedia near a "fundamental inflection point" to better compete with Airbnb and Booking.

title: As Travel Demand Soars, Buy Expedia and Wait on Airbnb, Analyst Says
text: Evercore analyst Mark Mahaney upgrades Expedia to Outperform and downgrades Airbnb to In Line.

title: Michael Burry's largest stock position surged 25% in a week
text: In the stock market, nothing speaks louder than numbers, and this week the traveling sector is almost deafening.

title: INTRODUCING UNPACK '24: THE TRENDS IN TRAVEL FROM EXPEDIA, HOTELS.COM AND VRBO
text: Pattaya, Paros and Perth Make the List of 2024 Destinations of the Year Tour Tourism, Dry Tripping and Go-ccasions Among 2024 Leading Trip Types Half of Travelers Expected to Adopt Generative AI in 2024 NEW YORK , Nov. 8, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Today, Expedia Group unveiled Unpack '24, a comprehensive look at what's motivating travelers and where they're going next year. Based on the company's unmatched first-party travel data, plus a global consumer survey of 20,000 travelers, Unpack '24 includes eight new travel trends for 2024, from top destinations to what's next in travel tech.

title: 5 Stocks That Powered S&P 500 ETF Last Week
text: After entering correction territory in the last week of October, the S&P 500 Index staged a solid comeback in the first week of November.

title: Expedia Shares Jump Over 18% After Reporting Record Revenue and Announcing a Stock Buyback
text: Booming travel demand lifted Expedia Group's (EXPE) sales to an all-time high, and the online travel site announced a new stock buyback plan. Shares spiked over 18% Friday following the news.

title: Expedia (EXPE) Q3 Earnings Beat Estimates, Revenues Rise Y/Y
text: Expedia's (EXPE) third-quarter 2023 results reflect the impact of strengthening travel demand.

title: Why Expedia Stock Rocketed Higher on Friday
text: Expedia had a standout quarter, breaking many records in the process. The company also announced a stock buyback that will reduce its outstanding share count significantly.

title: Michael Burry's third-biggest stock position soars 18% in a day
text: Earlier this year, the investing world was taken aback when legendary investor Michael Burry, known for his lucrative ‘Big Short' against the housing market in 2008, made another bearish bet, this time targeting US stocks.

title: Expedia (EXPE) Q3 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for Expedia (EXPE) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended September 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: Expedia Group: A $5 Billion Buyback Suggests That Michael Burry Could Be Right Again
text: Expedia Group's stock rose nearly 10% after announcing a new $5 billion share repurchase program. The company beat earnings and revenue expectations for Q3, driven by increased post-pandemic travel demand. Expedia's strong balance sheet and growth prospects make it an attractive investment, despite potential risks from competition and economic downturns.

title: Expedia Group Reports Record Revenue Amid Solid Travel Demand
text: Expedia Group reported record revenue in the third quarter amid solid travel demand. While travel demand in North America and Europe remained stable, the Asia-Pacific and Latin America regions experienced more pronounced growth, Expedia Group CEO Peter Kern said Thursday (Nov. 2) during the company's quarterly earnings call.

text: Expedia Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:EXPE ) Q3 2023 Earnings Conference Call November 2, 2023 4:30 PM ET Corporate Participants Harshit Vaish - SVP-Corporate Development, Strategy and Investor Relations Peter Kern - Vice Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Julie Whalen - Chief Financial Officer Conference Call Participants Eric Sheridan - Goldman Sachs Lee Horowitz - Deutsche Bank Jacob Hendricks - Cowen Naved Khan - B. title: Expedia (EXPE) Surpasses Q3 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Expedia (EXPE) came out with quarterly earnings of $5.41 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $5.15 per share. This compares to earnings of $4.05 per share a year ago.

title: Expedia's stock jumps 9% as company sees ‘resilience' in travel demand
text: Shares of Expedia Group Inc. EXPE, +1.68% rallied 9% in the after-hours session Thursday after the online travel company reported quarterly earnings well above Wall Street expectations, saying that demand for travel has been resilient. Expedia earned $425 million, or $2.87 a share, in the third quarter, compared with $482 million, or $2.98 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Countdown to Expedia (EXPE) Q3 Earnings: A Look at Estimates Beyond Revenue and EPS

title: What To Expect From Expedia's Q3 After Stock Up 8% This Year?
text: We expect Expedia's stock to likely trade higher with revenues and earnings beating consensus estimates marginally.

title: The Digital Face of Real Estate, Your Best Bets This Year
text: Traditional businesses typically become stagnant or outright 'boring' due to a combination of positive and negative factors. On the positive side, stagnation and stability come from the 'if it's not broken, don't fix it' attitude of doing things the same as before just because they've been working.
